children.label: A
children.text: within the coastal zone (as defined in section 304 of the Coastal Zone Management Act of 1972 ()) of the coastal State as of ; and16 U.S.C. 1453August 8, 2005

children.label: B
children.text: not more than 200 nautical miles from the geographic center of any leased tract.

children.heading: Coastal population
children.label: 2 Coastal population
children.text: The term “coastal population” means the population, as determined by the most recent official data of the Census Bureau, of each political subdivision any part of which lies within the designated coastal boundary of a State (as defined in a State’s coastal zone management program under the Coastal Zone Management Act of 1972 ( et seq.)).16 U.S.C. 1451

children.heading: Coastal State
children.label: 3 Coastal State
children.text: The term “coastal State” has the meaning given the term in section 304 of the Coastal Zone Management Act of 1972 ().16 U.S.C. 1453

children.heading: Coastline
children.label: 4 Coastline
children.text: The term “coastline” has the meaning given the term “coast line” in .section 1301 of this title

children.heading: Distance
children.label: 5 Distance
children.text: The term “distance” means the minimum great circle distance, measured in statute miles.

children.heading: Leased tract
children.label: 6 Leased tract
children.text: The term “leased tract” means a tract that is subject to a lease under section 1335 or 1337 of this title for the purpose of drilling for, developing, and producing oil or natural gas resources.

children.heading: Leasing moratoria
children.label: 7 Leasing moratoria
children.text: The term “leasing moratoria” means the prohibitions on preleasing, leasing, and related activities on any geographic area of the outer Continental Shelf as contained in sections 107 through 109 of division E of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2005 (; ).Public Law 108–447118 Stat. 3063

children.heading: Political subdivision
children.label: 8 Political subdivision
children.text: The term “political subdivision” means the local political jurisdiction immediately below the level of State government, including counties, parishes, and boroughs.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “producing State” means a coastal State that has a coastal seaward boundary within 200 nautical miles of the geographic center of a leased tract within any area of the outer Continental Shelf.

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: B Exclusion
children.text: The term “producing State” does not include a producing State, a majority of the coastline of which is subject to leasing moratoria, unless production was occurring on , from a lease within 10 nautical miles of the coastline of that State.January 1, 2005

children.label: I
children.text: seaward of the zone covered by ; orsection 1337(g) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: within that zone, but to which does not apply; andsection 1337(g) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the geographic center of which lies within a distance of 200 nautical miles from any part of the coastline of any coastal State.

children.heading: Inclusions
children.label: B Inclusions
children.text: The term “qualified Outer Continental Shelf revenues” includes bonus bids, rents, royalties (including payments for royalty taken in kind and sold), net profit share payments, and related late-payment interest from natural gas and oil leases issued under this subchapter.

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: C Exclusion
children.text: The term “qualified Outer Continental Shelf revenues” does not include any revenues from a leased tract or portion of a leased tract that is located in a geographic area subject to a leasing moratorium on , unless the lease was in production on .January 1, 2005January 1, 2005

children.heading: Payments to producing States and coastal political subdivisions
children.label: b Payments to producing States and coastal political subdivisions

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall, without further appropriation, disburse to producing States and coastal political subdivisions in accordance with this section $250,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2007 through 2010.

children.heading: Disbursement
children.label: 2 Disbursement
children.text: In each fiscal year, the Secretary shall disburse to each producing State for which the Secretary has approved a plan under subsection (c), and to coastal political subdivisions under paragraph (4), such funds as are allocated to the producing State or coastal political subdivision, respectively, under this section for the fiscal year.

children.label: i
children.text: the amount of qualified outer Continental Shelf revenues generated off the coastline of the producing State; bears to

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of qualified outer Continental Shelf revenues generated off the coastline of all producing States.

children.heading: Amount of outer Continental Shelf revenues
children.label: B Amount of outer Continental Shelf revenues
i
children.text: the amount of qualified outer Continental Shelf revenues for each of fiscal years 2007 and 2008 shall be determined using qualified outer Continental Shelf revenues received for fiscal year 2006; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of qualified outer Continental Shelf revenues for each of fiscal years 2009 and 2010 shall be determined using qualified outer Continental Shelf revenues received for fiscal year 2008.

children.label: i
children.text: the nearest point on the coastline of the producing State; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the geographic center of the leased tract.

children.heading: Minimum allocation
children.label: D Minimum allocation
children.text: The amount allocated to a producing State under subparagraph (A) shall be at least 1 percent of the amounts available under paragraph (1).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall pay 35 percent of the allocable share of each producing State, as determined under paragraph (3) to the coastal political subdivisions in the producing State.

children.label: I
children.text: the coastal population of the coastal political subdivision; bears to

children.label: II
children.text: the coastal population of all coastal political subdivisions in the producing State;

children.label: I
children.text: the number of miles of coastline of the coastal political subdivision; bears to

children.label: II
children.text: the number of miles of coastline of all coastal political subdivisions in the producing State; and

children.label: iii
children.text: 50 percent shall be allocated in amounts that are inversely proportional to the respective distances between the points in each coastal political subdivision that are closest to the geographic center of each leased tract, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Exception for the State of Louisiana
children.label: C Exception for the State of Louisiana
children.text: For the purposes of subparagraph (B)(ii), the coastline for coastal political subdivisions in the State of Louisiana without a coastline shall be considered to be ⅓ the average length of the coastline of all coastal political subdivisions with a coastline in the State of Louisiana.

children.heading: Exception for the State of Alaska
children.label: D Exception for the State of Alaska
children.text: For the purposes of carrying out subparagraph (B)(iii) in the State of Alaska, the amounts allocated shall be divided equally among the two coastal political subdivisions that are closest to the geographic center of a leased tract.

children.heading: Exclusion of certain leased tracts
children.label: E Exclusion of certain leased tracts
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (B)(iii), a leased tract or portion of a leased tract shall be excluded if the tract or portion of a leased tract is located in a geographic area subject to a leasing moratorium on , unless the lease was in production on that date.January 1, 2005

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B) and except as provided in subparagraph (C), in a case in which any amount allocated to a producing State or coastal political subdivision under paragraph (4) or (5) is not disbursed because the producing State does not have in effect a plan that has been approved by the Secretary under subsection (c), the Secretary shall allocate the undisbursed amount equally among all other producing States.

children.heading: Retention of allocation
children.label: B Retention of allocation
children.text: The Secretary shall hold in escrow an undisbursed amount described in subparagraph (A) until such date as the final appeal regarding the disapproval of a plan submitted under subsection (c) is decided.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: C Waiver
children.text: The Secretary may waive subparagraph (A) with respect to an allocated share of a producing State and hold the allocable share in escrow if the Secretary determines that the producing State is making a good faith effort to develop and submit, or update, a plan in accordance with subsection (c).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than , the Governor of a producing State shall submit to the Secretary a coastal impact assistance plan.July 1, 2008

children.heading: Public participation
children.label: B Public participation
children.text: In carrying out subparagraph (A), the Governor shall solicit local input and provide for public participation in the development of the plan.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall approve a plan of a producing State submitted under paragraph (1) before disbursing any amount to the producing State, or to a coastal political subdivision located in the producing State, under this section.

children.label: i
children.text: the Secretary determines that the plan is consistent with the uses described in subsection (d); and

children.label: I
children.text: the name of the State agency that will have the authority to represent and act on behalf of the producing State in dealing with the Secretary for purposes of this section;

children.label: II
children.text: a program for the implementation of the plan that describes how the amounts provided under this section to the producing State will be used;

children.label: aa
children.text: the name of a contact person; and

children.label: bb
children.text: a description of how the coastal political subdivision will use amounts provided under this section;

children.label: IV
children.text: a certification by the Governor that ample opportunity has been provided for public participation in the development and revision of the plan; and

children.label: V
children.text: a description of measures that will be taken to determine the availability of assistance from other relevant Federal resources and programs.

children.label: A
children.text: developed in accordance with this subsection; and

children.label: B
children.text: submitted to the Secretary for approval or disapproval under paragraph (4).

children.heading: Procedure
children.label: 4 Procedure
children.text: Not later than 90 days after the date on which a plan or amendment to a plan is submitted under paragraph (1) or (3), the Secretary shall approve or disapprove the plan or amendment.

children.label: A
children.text: Projects and activities for the conservation, protection, or restoration of coastal areas, including wetland.

children.label: B
children.text: Mitigation of damage to fish, wildlife, or natural resources.

children.label: C
children.text: Planning assistance and the administrative costs of complying with this section.

children.label: D
children.text: Implementation of a federally-approved marine, coastal, or comprehensive conservation management plan.

children.label: E
children.text: Mitigation of the impact of outer Continental Shelf activities through funding of onshore infrastructure projects and public service needs.

children.heading: Compliance with authorized uses
children.label: 2 Compliance with authorized uses
children.text: If the Secretary determines that any expenditure made by a producing State or coastal political subdivision is not consistent with this subsection, the Secretary shall not disburse any additional amount under this section to the producing State or the coastal political subdivision until such time as all amounts obligated for unauthorized uses have been repaid or reobligated for authorized uses.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 3 Limitation
children.text: Not more than 23 percent of amounts received by a producing State or coastal political subdivision for any 1 fiscal year shall be used for the purposes described in subparagraphs (C) and (E) of paragraph (1).

children.label: A
children.text: consistent with subsection (d); and

children.label: B
children.text: specifically designed to respond to the spill of national significance.

children.heading: Approval by Secretary
children.label: 2 Approval by Secretary
children.text: The Secretary may, in the sole discretion of the Secretary, approve, on a project by project basis, the immediate disbursal of the funds under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Additional information
children.label: A Additional information
children.text: If the Secretary approves a project for funding under this subsection that is included in a plan previously approved under subsection (c), not later than 90 days after the date of the funding approval, the producing State or coastal political subdivision shall submit to the Secretary any additional information that the Secretary determines to be necessary to ensure that the project is in compliance with subsection (d).

children.heading: Amendment to plan
children.label: B Amendment to plan
children.text: If the Secretary approves a project for funding under this subsection that is not included in a plan previously approved under subsection (c), not later than 90 days after the date of the funding approval, the producing State or coastal political subdivision shall submit to the Secretary for approval an amendment to the plan that includes any projects funded under paragraph (1), as well as any information about such projects that the Secretary determines to be necessary to ensure that the project is in compliance with subsection (d).

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: C Limitation
children.text: If a producing State or coastal political subdivision does not submit the additional information or amendments to the plan required by this paragraph, or if, based on the information submitted by the Secretary determines that the project is not in compliance with subsection (d), by the deadlines specified in this paragraph, the Secretary shall not disburse any additional funds to the producing State or the coastal political subdivisions until the date on which the additional information or amendment to the plan has been approved by the Secretary.
